A dropped coin rings in a narrow alley just as shadows shift at both ends. The thieves mean to frighten, grab, and vanish into the crowd.
The granary floor moves in a rippling mass beneath torn sacks. Hungry rats pour from the walls when the door opens.
A watcher at a shuttered inn recognizes the party too late and reaches for a hidden blade. Papers on the table begin to burn.
A side lane through a market can save time if the party reads the flow of carts, porters, and guards before stepping into it.
A small iron key lies in the gutter below a moneychanger's window. Finding the matching box is another problem entirely.
A cart breaks loose on the slope, scattering apples and shouting vendors. A fast dodge avoids wheels, hooves, and splintered wood.
A vendor's bruised fruit crate is headed for the refuse pile. Sorting fast saves the good pieces before they are spoiled.
Abandoned rooftop pots hold hardy herbs above the street smoke. The stairs are rotten, but the herbs are clean.
A bakery has spilled clean grain from a split sack beside the back door. Quick hands can gather it before rain or rats arrive.
Perch gather beneath a canal bridge where crumbs and shade are plentiful. The water is murky, but the bites come fast.
Small pilchards flash around harbor pilings, quick enough to avoid lazy nets. A tight cast can fill a basket.
Fat carp circle in an old public fountain where no one admits to feeding them. Catching one means avoiding the watchful square.
A mason's yard has a pile of rejected stone offcuts, many still useful for small work. Permission, timing, or charm decides how easy this is.
A kiln yard has cracked bricks set aside as seconds. The sound ones can still serve if sorted from the ruined batch.
A torn copper gutter hangs from a storm-damaged roof. Bringing it down safely matters more than reaching it.
Broken crates are stacked behind a warehouse, their labels scraped away. The long boards can be pried loose before the yardmaster returns.
A street tree has fallen after a night storm, blocking a lane and annoying everyone. Cutting it into manageable lengths earns more thanks than coin.
A work crew has abandoned weathered scaffold planks beside a half-repaired wall. The straight pieces are buried beneath tangled rope.
A knife crew melts from alley doors after the last cart passes. Their leader offers directions while the others block the exits.
A locked cellar beneath a respectable shop scratches from within. When opened, severed hands spill over the threshold in a frantic swarm.
A pawnshop suit of armor steps off its stand when a bell rings behind the counter. The proprietor swears it was only display yesterday.
Chalk marks on a wall show the Watch changed patrol routes after trouble nearby. Reading the code reveals which alley is watched and which is bait.
A chase crosses a roof of loose red tiles. Quick footing keeps the slope from turning into a noisy fall into the street.
A silk reticule lies under a market stall with a broken clasp and too many coins for an accident. A tiny crest is sewn inside.
A market stall has set aside bruised but good produce before closing. Sorting quickly yields food before sweepers clear the square.
A temple garden offers mint to travelers who leave a small prayer. The healthiest sprigs grow near the fountain stones.
Perch gather beneath lantern reflections in a city canal. The line must avoid boat traffic and floating rubbish.
A toppled fishmonger's barrel spills live eels into the gutter. Catching them saves the merchant a ruined evening.
A crab basket tied below the quay is heavy with catch and tangled rope. Freeing it without losing the line takes careful hands.
A stack of cracked roof slate sits behind a guildhall after repairs. Several sheets remain whole enough to salvage.
A cracked handbell from a street shrine lies in a refuse cart. The bronze is clean if separated before the cart leaves.
Discarded crates pile behind the docks after a busy market day. The best boards still bear dry trade marks.
A scaffold crew has stacked damaged but usable planks beside an alley. Permission, coin, or timing might secure a few.
A broken shop sign of painted oak lies in the street after a brawl. Its back is unpainted and still dry.
A cracked crate behind a tavern has sprouted pale mushrooms in damp straw. The edible caps are firm and smell faintly of bread.
A cobbler's alley bin holds bent nails, buckles, and iron heel plates. Sorting the useful scrap takes time before the sweepers arrive.
